subject: An @auditor finding (or a full audit report) produced against an actor's draft -- a compliance/local-law/data-integrity adversarial pass carrying the Hypothesis+Falsification+Confidence triple.

dimensions.desc: States a single, specific predicted failure mode in one sentence -- not a vague worry ('this might be wrong somewhere').
Names the exact check run (a grep, a re-quote, a recomputation, a compliance-skill cross-reference) plus its literal result -- not 'I checked and it seemed fine'.
Confidence (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) matches the strength of the evidence given -- HIGH only for a structurally-verifiable disagreement, LOW pushed to open questions rather than dressed up as a finding.
When the concern is compliance/local-law, the finding grounds any jurisdiction or regulation claim in a named source rather than asserting law from memory.

guidance: Reward a finding a third party could independently re-run and get the same falsification result from, with confidence honestly calibrated to the evidence and any legal/jurisdictional claim explicitly sourced. Penalize a vague hypothesis with no concrete check, an unfalsifiable claim dressed up with a HIGH confidence label, and any compliance assertion presented as fact with no grounding.
